title: The Wind and the Sun
content:
One fine day, the Wind and the Sun were having a loud argument. "I am the strongest power in the whole sky!" boasted the Wind. "No," said the Sun calmly. "I am the strongest." They decided to have a contest to settle the matter.

Far below on a dusty road, a traveler was walking along wearing a thick, warm coat. "The one who can make that traveler take off his coat first is the strongest," said the Sun. The Wind agreed eagerly and went first.

The Wind took a deep breath and blew with all his might. He howled and roared and sent the trees bending low. The traveler shivered and clutched his coat tightly! The harder the Wind blew, the more firmly the traveler held on. At last the Wind gave up, exhausted and embarrassed.

Now it was the Sun's turn. The Sun smiled and began to shine with a warm, steady light. The traveler relaxed and undid a button on his coat. The Sun shone warmer. The traveler undid another button. Warmer still, and the traveler pulled the coat right off and sat down happily in the sunshine. Gentleness is always stronger than force.

author: Aesop
